title: Notification badge does not clear after inbox is read
bug_description: The unread notification badge stays visible after the inbox items are marked read, making the UI state stale.
expected_behavior: Once the inbox items are read and the read state sync succeeds, the notification badge should clear immediately.
actual_behavior: The inbox content updates but the badge remains non-zero until a full page refresh.

preconditions: Frontend dev server is running with a seeded account that has unread notifications
The inbox page is reachable in the local environment
steps: Open the inbox page with unread items
Mark all items as read or open the newest unread item
Observe the notification badge in the header without refreshing the page
reproduction.expected_behavior: The badge should update to zero or the new unread count immediately.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The badge stays stale until the page or route is refreshed.

logs: Client state log shows the read mutation succeeds but the badge selector does not recompute

manual_checks: Read the final unread item and confirm the badge clears without a full page reload
Switch away and back to the inbox route to confirm no stale state reappears

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if the badge stops updating for new unread messages.
rollback.notes: Preserve the last known-good client state sync implementation for quick restore.

scope_notes: Keep the change inside inbox read-state handling, badge derivation, and related tests. Do not refactor unrelated header or websocket code.

notes.root_cause_hint: The unread-count selector may depend on stale cached state or miss a read-mutation invalidation step.
notes.risk_hint: A broad cache reset could hide the bug but cause flicker or break other notification counts.
similar_bugs: header count not recomputed after mutation
client cache invalidation missing after mark-as-read
